g.data-narration: SNMP is the standard protocol for managing network devices, essential for IoT and M2M systems.
SNMP is an application-layer protocol that enables administrators to monitor, manage, and control network devices remotely.
SNMP uses a manager-agent model where the manager monitors and controls agents on managed devices through the network.
The core components are Manager, Agent, MIB database, and OIDs - each plays a specific role in network management.
GET retrieves data from MIB objects; SET modifies them. These are the primary operations for monitoring and configuration.
TRAP is an asynchronous alert sent by agents to managers without acknowledgment. INFORM requires acknowledgment for reliability.
GETNEXT traverses MIB tables sequentially, while GETBULK efficiently retrieves large amounts of data in one request.
g.text: Sequential access • One object at a time
Bulk retrieval • Optimized for large tables
Example: GET all 100 sensor readings from a gateway in one request

g.data-narration: Three SNMP versions exist: v1 (original), v2c (enhanced), and v3 (secure). Each offers different features and security models.
SNMPv1/v2c use plaintext community strings - insecure. SNMPv3 uses USM with SHA authentication and AES encryption.
In IoT, SNMP monitors sensors via GET, receives TRAP alerts for faults, configures devices via SET, and tracks performance.
g.text: ⚠ Lightweight agents • Custom MIBs • Use v3 for security

g.data-narration: SNMP is simple and lightweight using UDP, suitable for monitoring. NETCONF-YANG offers full CRUD operations over secure SSH.
g.text: IoT: SNMP for constrained devices • NETCONF for gateways

g.data-narration: In practice, the manager queries a sensor's OID, the agent reads from MIB, and returns the temperature value to display.
g.text: EXAMPLE: READING TEMPERATURE FROM IoT SENSOR

g.data-narration: Key takeaways: SNMP uses manager-agent model, has GET/SET/TRAP operations, v3 adds security, and is essential for IoT management.
g.text: Manager-Agent Model • GET/SET/GETBULK/TRAP/INFORM • MIB & OID Hierarchy
v3 = Authentication (SHA) + Encryption (AES) • SNMP for lightweight IoT monitoring
